name: Fastly API
what_it_does: Programmable CDN and edge cloud platform with a REST API for managing CDN services, VCL configurations, edge compute (Compute@Edge with Wasm/WASI), purging, and real-time analytics.
use_cases: Programmatic cache purging by URL, surrogate key, or tag from deployment pipelines
Deploying and configuring VCL (Varnish Configuration Language) edge logic via API
Managing WAF rules and security configurations programmatically
Configuring edge compute (WebAssembly) deployments for custom request handling
Retrieving real-time CDN traffic analytics and cache hit ratios
not_for: Teams needing a simple plug-and-play CDN without VCL knowledge
Small traffic volumes where Cloudflare or Bunny.net is sufficient
Static asset hosting only (overkill; use S3 + CloudFront)
best_when: You're a large-scale web application requiring programmable edge logic, custom caching strategies, and API-driven CDN configuration with real-time purging.
avoid_when: You need a simple, low-cost CDN without custom edge logic or VCL expertise.

auth.notes: API tokens with scoped permissions (purge, global, global:read). Tokens can be restricted to specific services. User API tokens and automation tokens are distinct. HMAC for webhook validation.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier; developer trial available
pricing.paid_tiers: Pay-as-you-go from $0.01/GB; enterprise contracts with volume discounts
pricing.notes: Usage-based pricing on bandwidth, compute, and requests. Enterprise customers typically negotiate contracts. Fastly targets mid-to-large companies.

performance.rate_limits: API: 1000 requests/second per token; purge: rate limited per service

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Errors include msg and detail fields. API versioning is clear. Purge responses indicate cache cleared vs. already empty. Generally informative.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Purge operations are idempotent — purging already-purged content succeeds. Configuration changes require versioning — Fastly uses service versions that must be activated.
known_agent_gotchas: All Fastly service configuration changes require creating a new version and activating it — no in-place edits
VCL configuration requires VCL expertise — programming the edge is not like a typical REST API
Compute@Edge (Wasm) deployments require a separate build and upload workflow
Purge-by-tag (surrogate keys) requires setting Surrogate-Key headers on origin responses
Real-time analytics has a 2-minute delay for aggregated data
Service versions are immutable once active — cloning is required to modify a live configuration
